author: parithosh
content: if i have a machine with more than 1 GPU on a single host, would the prover use all GPUs?

author: somnergy
content:
There is also an upcoming airbender version of this that would very easily support multi-gpu.

This one, unfortunately, would not. Only way i can think of how to do that is to pass to docker a different GPU index and run these staggered.
There is a `--start-block` option (all options can be fetched with `z6m_prover -h`. So i would do `--start-block N --prove-every 2` with docker passed gpu index 0, and `--start-block N+1 --prove-every 2` for odd numbered blocks and so on.

There is sp1-cluster that has support for multi-gpu, i haven't had time to test it out, it had some issues.
